title: message_set readAt: client-supplied timestamp stored verbatim without RFC 3339 validation
description: jmap.rs message_set update, readAt path: Phase 1 checks only that the value is a string (value.as_str().is_some()), not that it is a valid timestamp. Phase 2 passes the raw string to store.read_message(msg_id, ts) which writes it to the read_at column. An authenticated client can store any string in read_at (e.g. 'not-a-date', SQL injection attempt — ineffective via parameterized query but stored as garbage). Comparing read_at to datetime() functions in future queries would silently fail or produce wrong results. Suggested fix: validate with chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339 before accepting, same as senderExpiresAt.
